---
title: "About this prototype"
---

## What this is

A static, open-source prototype that analyzes the US economy in 2026 against
the prior 25 years and compares economic fundamentals to market performance.
Every chart is an interactive JavaScript widget rendered into a self-contained
HTML page — no server, no database, no paid libraries.

## Stack

| Piece | Role | License |
|---|---|---|
| [Quarto](https://quarto.org) | Authoring + site builder | MIT |
| [echarts4r](https://echarts4r.john-coene.com/) → [Apache ECharts](https://echarts.apache.org/) | Drill-down charts (sunburst, treemap, bars, lines) | Apache 2.0 |
| [plotly](https://plotly-r.com/) | Linked scatter / hover charts | MIT |
| [reactable](https://glin.github.io/reactable/) | Interactive tables with expandable rows | MIT |
| [sparkline](https://github.com/htmlwidgets/sparkline) | Inline mini-charts | BSD-3 |
| [crosstalk](https://rstudio.github.io/crosstalk/) | Client-side linked filtering | MIT |

## Data

**This prototype is a *calibrated synthetic* dataset, not live data.**

- Annual values for 2001–2024 are hand-entered from public sources: BEA
  (GDP, GDP components), BLS (unemployment, CPI), Federal Reserve (Fed Funds,
  10Y Treasury), NBER (recession dating), and public index aggregates
  (S&P 500, Dow, NASDAQ, VIX). Exact vintage-adjusted official values may
  differ slightly.
- Values for 2025 and 2026 are *illustrative prototype values*. They are not
  forecasts and should not be treated as such. Any year ≥ 2025 is flagged
  with `prototype = 1` in the CSV and marked with an asterisk in the UI.
- Quarterly and monthly series are decomposed from the annual series with
  seeded noise so that quarterly/monthly averages match the annual figure.

### Swapping in live data

`R/generate_data.R` is the only place data is produced. To use live FRED data:

1. `install.packages("fredr")` and set a FRED API key via `fredr_set_key()`.
2. Replace the annual `tibble::tribble(...)` block with:

   ```r
   fetch <- function(id) fredr::fredr(series_id = id, frequency = "a")
   annual <- reduce(list(
     fetch("GDPC1")  |> transmute(year = year(date), gdp_growth  = value),
     fetch("UNRATE") |> transmute(year = year(date), unemployment = value),
     fetch("CPIAUCSL") |> transmute(year = year(date), cpi = value),
     fetch("FEDFUNDS") |> transmute(year = year(date), fed_funds = value),
     fetch("DGS10")  |> transmute(year = year(date), ten_year = value),
     fetch("SP500")  |> transmute(year = year(date), sp500_ret = value)
   ), full_join, by = "year")
   ```

3. Re-run `Rscript R/generate_data.R`, then `quarto render`.

## Regenerating the site

```sh
Rscript R/generate_data.R   # rebuild CSVs (only needed if data changes)
quarto render               # rebuild HTML into _site/
```

## Publishing

A GitHub Actions workflow at `.github/workflows/publish.yml` runs on every
push to `main`, renders the site, and pushes `_site/` to the `gh-pages`
branch. Enable Pages in repo settings to serve from `gh-pages`.

## Known limitations

- **Synthetic 2025–2026 values** — see above.
- **Quarterly/monthly series are interpolated**, not actual vintage data.
  High-frequency movements within a year are illustrative only.
- **No intraday data** — daily and intraday series would require a larger
  dataset than is useful for a static prototype.
- **No nominal/real adjustment** is done on market returns; they are reported
  as nominal total returns.
